title: Unicode Normalization, Case Folding, and Original Evidence · Runtime Lens: Original Normalized Pair
summary: Visually identical words count separately, compatibility characters collapse without disclosure, lowercasing misses caseless matches, and the report cannot link normalized tokens back to original text. Predict the user decision, input identity, text policy, expected report, failure boundary, and evidence that could disprove the proposed implementation before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the original normalized pair increment in versioned reversible Unicode poli…
objectives: Retain original spans beside normalized comparison keys so reports remain inspectable and reversible.
Use casefold for declared caseless matching and test expansions and language-sensitive limitations rather than assuming lower is equivalent.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-bookbot-python cumulative Unicode-safe text-analysis portfolio.

Browser execution uses original fixed in-memory fixtures and deterministic pure-Python models only.
Host files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, package installation, native profiling, load,
operating-system races, publication, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class TextPolicy:
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    normalization: str = "NFC"
    casefold: bool = True
    top_k: int = 10


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    corpus_id: str
    revision: str
    policy_version: str
    command_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Bookbot portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Worked focus: bookbot-original-normalized-pair — Retain original spans beside normalized comparison keys so reports remain inspectable and reversible.
# Evidence: bookbot-worked-bookbot-unicode-normalization-2
# Competency: bookbot-worked-bookbot-unicode-normalization-2.
# Case 7417: an assistive-technology terminal review.
# Operating constraint: record the first failed boundary, causal evidence, repair, regression test, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner.
# Changed case: replace an ASCII word with a canonically equivalent combining sequence and compare the declared counts.
def worked_bookbot_unicode_normalization_2(original_units: int = 9, normalized_units: int = 8, form: str = "NFC", original_kept: bool = True, unicode_version_kept: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked2 = "worked2-2dekrow-7417"
    assert evidence_variant_worked2
    evidence_axes_worked2 = (
        "corpusworked2",
        "policy2dekrow",
        "revision7417",
        "commandworked27417",
        "report2dekrow7417",
        "test7417worked2",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked2)) == 6
    if original_units < 0 or normalized_units < 0:
        return False, normalized_units, "unicode-count"
    if form not in {"NFC", "NFD", "NFKC", "NFKD", "NONE"}:
        return False, normalized_units, "normalization-form"
    if not original_kept:
        return False, normalized_units, "lost-evidence"
    if not unicode_version_kept:
        return False, normalized_units, "unversioned-policy"
    assert original_units >= 0
    return True, normalized_units, "reversible-unicode"

At Bookbot and Python Defend the Original Normalized Pair model · Theory Original Normalized Pair, trace this concrete input and predict admission, decoding, normalization, tokenization, counting, ranking, formatting, stdout, stderr, status, test, package, failure, and recovery evidence before reviewing it: A theory Bookbot team handles case 3065 during an offline public-library reading report. Build deterministic Python evidence to version normalization and case policy while preserving inspectable original text; keep browser work pure, deterministic, bounded, and free of host file, process, Git, package-install, credential, and release effects; then change LF to CRLF, remove the final newline, and preserve the declared word and line policy. Browser code uses pure bounded functions and original fixed fixtures only. Real files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, packages, installations, native profiling, operating-system races, load, publication, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Retain original spans beside normalized comparison keys so reports remain inspectable and reversible.

For Bookbot and Python Defend the Original Normalized Pair model · Theory Original Normalized Pair, challenge the documented misconception with changed-file, changed-encoding, Unicode, token, ranking, stream, CLI, test, package, security, accessibility, failure, recovery, and controlled native-transfer evidence: Once counts are correct, original spelling and offsets no longer matter.
